title: Café Kitchen Counter Designer
description: The user designs a modern café kitchen counter display by placing and arranging avocados and a coffee machine on an architectural countertop. Users can add multiple avocados, adjust their positions along the counter, rotate the coffee machine, toggle ambient and spot lighting presets, and validate the final layout meets specific spacing and placement rules before exporting the design.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive café kitchen counter designer. Load both GLB assets using GLTFLoader: shared_assets/Avocado.glb and shared_assets/coffeemat.glb. Scene setup: Create a long rectangular box (6x0.1x1.5 units) as a marble-textured countertop at y=1, with a back wall plane behind it. Place the coffeemat (coffee machine) on the right side of the counter, scaled appropriately. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 3, 5) looking at the counter center. Add ambient light (intensity 0.3), one directional light simulating a window, and two point lights above the counter as pendant lamps. Interactions (6-10 steps with validation): 1) Click an 'Add Avocado' button to spawn an avocado on the counter at a random x-position (max 5 avocados). 2) Click and drag any avocado along the counter x-axis to reposition it, constrained to counter bounds. 3) Click the coffee machine to cycle through 3 rotation angles (0, 45, -45 degrees on Y-axis). 4) A 'Toggle Lighting' button cycles through 3 presets: daylight, evening warm, and spotlight-only. 5) A 'Scale Counter' slider adjusts counter length between 4-8 units, repositioning items proportionally. 6) A 'Validate Layout' button checks: all avocados must be at least 0.4 units apart from each other, no avocado overlaps the coffee machine zone (rightmost 1.5 units), and at least 2 avocados must be placed. Display pass/fail with specific error messages in an overlay div. 7) An 'Export View' button captures the current camera angle description to a text overlay. Use OrbitControls for camera navigation. Add a subtle grid helper below the counter. Include a visible HTML panel on the left with all buttons and the slider. Raycasting must be used for object selection and dragging.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  avocadoCount: number,         // current number of avocados placed on counter (0-5)
  avocadoPositions: string,         // JSON array of x-positions for each avocado on counter
  coffeeMachineRotation: number,         // current Y-axis rotation of coffee machine in degrees (0, 45, or -45)
  lightingPreset: number,         // current lighting mode index (0=daylight, 1=evening, 2=spotlight)
  counterLength: number,         // current length of the counter in units (4.0-8.0)
  ambientIntensity: number,         // current ambient light intensity (0.0-1.0)
  directionalIntensity: number,         // current directional light intensity (0.0-1.0)
  pointLightsActive: boolean,         // whether point lights above counter are enabled
  isDragging: boolean,         // whether user is currently dragging an avocado
  selectedAvocadoIndex: number,         // index of currently selected/dragged avocado (-1 if none)
  totalInteractions: number,         // cumulative count of all user interactions performed
}
